zh.Hawridge, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國巴金哈姆爾霍利其
zh.Prittlewell, Southend-on-Sea Unitary Aut: 英國英格蘭艾可普利特列維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Ann Marshall於1400年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾時，她的父親John Delta Marshall30歲，母親Marie Lamplugh25歲。她於1420年與Philip Harris在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1450年之後在英國德比夏爾。

zh-hans.Hawridge, Buckinghamshire, England: 英国巴金哈姆爾霍利其
zh-hans.Prittlewell, Southend-on-Sea Unitary Aut: 英国英格兰艾可普利特列維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary Ann Marshall于1400年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾时，她的父亲John Delta Marshall30岁，母亲Marie Lamplugh25岁。她于1420年与Philip Harris在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1450年之后在英国德比夏爾。

nameMeanings.name: Marshall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: usually an occupational name ‘farrier’, occasionally a status name ‘chief official of a royal household or court; a high officer of state’, from Middle English mareshal and Old French maresc(h)al. An even wider range of meanings is found in some other languages: compare for example Polish Marszałek (see Marszalek ). This name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. It is also borne by Jews, presumably as an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.
Americanized form of German   Marschall  .
Americanized form of French   Mercier  .

variants.name: Marchel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jacek, Ryszard.  German Ernst.

Polish: probably from French maréchal or Old French maresc(h)al, which was originally an occupational name for someone who was in charge of the horses of a household, later a military rank for a senior general. See Marshall and compare Marszalek .

variants.name: Mercier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Gaston, Lucien, Aurele, Jacques, Normand, Laurent, Michel, Valmont.

French and Channel Islands (Jersey): occupational name for a merchant, from Old French mercier, from Late Latin merciarius (an agent derivative of merx, genitive mercis ‘merchandise’). This name was also taken to England by Huguenot refugees. Compare Marshall and Mercy .

variants.name: Maniscalco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Vito, Sal, Antonio, Ignazio, Carmelo, Francesco, Gaspare, Saverio, Antonino, Carlo, Damiano.

Italian (southern): occupational name from maniscalco ‘farrier’, altered from marescalco by folk etymology, probably influenced by mano ‘hand’, from medieval Latin mariscalcus, marescalcus. Compare Marshall .

variants.name: Marchal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gilles, Jacques, Maryse, Philippe, Pierre, Veronique.

French and Walloon: status name or occupational name from Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The term is of ancient Germanic origin (from marah ‘horse, mare’ + scalc ‘servant’) and was originally applied to a man who looked after horses. In the Middle Ages it also came to be used on the one hand as an occupational name for a farrier, and on the other as a status name for an officer of state, in particular a member of a royal household with military responsibilities. Compare English Marshall and 2 below.
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland, and is found mainly in Glamorgan, Wales.

variants.name: Maskell
meanings: English: variant of Marshall , derived from Middle English, Old French maresc(h)al ‘marshal’. The first element in the placename Mascallsbury (Essex) derives from the surname of a 13th-century individual named Robert le Marescal.

variants.name: Marszalek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Piotr, Alicja, Andrzej, Eugeniusz, Henryk, Jerzy, Kazimierz, Mieczyslaw, Wieslaw, Zbigniew, Zofia, Zygmunt.

Polish (Marszałek): occupational or status name from the vocabulary word marszałek ‘marshal’, denoting anything from a high court official, a field marshal, or the chairman of the Polish parliament, to the senior servant in a great household. Compare English Marshall .

variants.name: Marshell
meanings: English: variant of   Marshall  .

variants.name: Marschall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Hedwig, Juergen, Reinhard, Theresia, Wilfried.

German: occupational name for a man in the service of a member of the nobility, originally one who looked after the horses, Middle High German marschalc (from mar(c) ‘noble horse’ + schalk ‘servant’). In time the term came to be used for a military rank or a certain court official. Compare Marshall .
English: variant of   Marshall  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marshal
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Marshall  .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Ann Marshall was born in 1400, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Delta Marshall, was 30 and her mother, Marie Lamplugh, was 25. She married Philip Harris in 1420, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1450, in Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Ann Marshall est née en 1400, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Delta Marshall, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Marie Lamplugh, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Philip Harris en 1420, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1450, à Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Ann Marshall 1400 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Delta Marshall 30 und ihre Mutter Marie Lamplugh 25. Sie hat 1420 in Essex, England, United Kingdom Philip Harris geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1450 in Derbyshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Ann Marshall è nata il nel 1400 nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Delta Marshall, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Marie Lamplugh, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Philip Harris nel 1420, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1450, nel Derbyshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Ann Marshallが1400年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父John Delta Marshallは30歳，母Marie Lamplughは25歳であった。1420年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでPhilip Harrisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1450年以後にイギリスデルブイシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Ann Marshall(이)가 1400년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Delta Marshall(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie Lamplugh(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Philip Harris(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1450년 이후 영국 더비실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Ann Marshall nasceu em 1400, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Delta Marshall, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Marie Lamplugh, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Philip Harris em 1420, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1450, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Ann Marshall родилась в 1400 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Delta Marshall было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie Lamplugh, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip Harris в 1420 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1450 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Ann Marshall nació en 1400, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Delta Marshall, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Marie Lamplugh, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Philip Harris en 1420, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1450, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra.
